name: rf/series_parallel_rlc
description: Series RLC one-port: FDTD resonance peak vs analytic f0 = 1/(2pi sqrt(LC)).
falsification: EXECUTED: a valid RLC-resonance bench must track C: doubling C should lower the peak by sqrt(2). Measured ratio 1.030 vs sqrt(2)=1.414 shows it does NOT -- the peak is parasitic, so this gate correctly stays red.
notes: FDTD load-port current peak at 7.901 GHz (C=1pF) vs analytic 7.118 GHz, and 7.669 GHz at C=2pF. The C(1)->C(2) peak ratio is 1.030 vs ideal sqrt(2)=1.414: the lumped two-port bench is parasitic-dominated and does NOT isolate the RLC resonance. Analytic f0 binds as the first-line reference; the wave-level RLC resonance from a propagating transmission structure is an OPEN GAP (tests/rf/wave_validation/test_rlc_resonance_wave_level.py, xfail strict).
